summary: The proposed drug DN501 functions through the same primary mechanism as darunavir—protease inhibition—which targets and blocks the HIV protease enzyme essential for virus replication. This shared mechanism suggests similar antiviral efficacy while potentially offering improved safety profiles due to fewer adverse events like hyperglycemia.
